channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Nintendo Indie World March 2026 Showcase - All the News and Announcements
item.content:encoded:
Today's Nintendo Indie World showcase contained around 15 minutes of info about upcoming indies for Switch 2 and Switch, and here's everything that was announced during that show!

Denshattack! is basically Yellow Bus Goes Vroom

We begin with a release date for the zany-looking bus platformer (yes, really) Denshattack!, which comes to Switch 2 on June 17th. You can check out a demo today.

My Little Puppy looks too adorable for words

Next, we get a look at My Little Puppy, a frankly heartbreakingly adorable game about reuniting with your cherished pup in the afterlife. It's coming to Switch 2 and Switch on May 29th.

Heave Ho 2 brings more friend-flinging shenanigans

If you enjoyed the first Heave Ho for its mixture of gleeful physics-based fun and infuriating friendship ruination, Heave Ho 2 is the game for you, and you can play it on both Switch 2 and Switch in summer.

Moonlighter 2: The Endless Vault is coming to Switch 2

Moonlighter 2 will be bringing its blend of dungeon-crawling action and shop management to the Switch 2 sometime this year, Digital Sun revealed during today's show.

Woodo lets you build charming wooden dioramas

Do you love Unpacking? Would you like a chance to play something similar, but with a gorgeous wood-based aesthetic instead? Well, Woodo is coming to both Switch 2 and Switch in summer.

Minishoot' Adventures should scratch that Zelda itch

2024's excellent bullet hell shooter-come-Zelda explore-'em-up Minishoot' Adventures is coming to Switch 2 and Switch. When, you ask? Later today, I say!

The Midnight Walk is a stop-motion horror adventure from Lost in Random's developer

Our next game was The Midnight Walk, a stop-motion horror game that mixes The Nightmare Before Christmas with developer Zoink's previous game Lost in Random. It's coming to Switch 2 on March 26th.

Rotwood brings Klei's beautiful brawler to Switch 2

Here's another game you'll be able to play sooner than you might think. Rotwood is a side-scrolling brawler that comes to us from Klei Entertainment, and it'll be on Switch 2 later today!

Mixtape should appeal to Stranger Things fans

Next up, it's a look at Mixtape, a game that looks to be indebted to the likes of Netflix's hit show Stranger Things and Don't Nod's Lost Records: Bloom & Rage. It comes to Switch 2 on May 7th!

Blighted is a "psychedelic Western nightmare" from Guacamelee's Drinkbox

Drinkbox brings its signature hand-animated-esque aesthetic to Blighted, an action RPG with Metroidvania elements that you can enjoy either solo or co-op. Look forward to this one on Switch 2 this fall.

Deadzone: Rogue introduces sci-fi roguelike action to the Switch 2

If some of the games shown during today's Indie World showcase have been a little bit too twee for you, Deadzone: Rogue should do the job. It's a sci-fi roguelike shooter coming to Switch 2 on March 17th.

It's sizzle reel time, baby!

Next up, it's time for a sizzle reel! Here's everything we got to see during the next rapid-fire round of games coming to Switch 2 and Switch.

  • Grave Seasons (Switch - summer 2026)
  • Unrailed 2: Back on Track (Switch 2 and Switch - May 2026)
  • Toem 2 (Switch - summer 2026)
  • inKonbini: One Store. Many Stories. (Switch 2 and Switch - April 30th)
  • Outbound (Switch 2 and Switch - April 23rd)
  • Ratatan (Switch 2 - July 16th)

Blue Prince can puzzle you all over again on Switch 2

The show closed with a look at Blue Prince, which is bringing its head-scratching puzzling to Switch 2...later today! This was my game of the year for 2025, so I'd very much recommend checking it out.

That's all she wrote! Join us for the next Nintendo Direct show, whenever it may be.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/nintendo-indie-world-march-2026-news-and-announcements
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dungeons & Dragons 2026 Roadmap Promises Seasons of Horror and Magic
item.content:encoded:
Now, into March of 2026, there's been a lingering question for fans of Dungeons & Dragons and that's "What's next?" Today at GAMA: The Tabletop Game Association, Wizards of the Coast broke the silence to announce a number of products for the 2026 through September including Sourcebooks, Adventures, and a variety of tools to help DMs and players grouped together in seasons.

With the introduction of seasons, Senior VP Dan Ayoub explained that book releases will work to 'anchor' seasons with a specific theme so that at the same time a book is releasing, there are complementary products being made available at the same time.

The first three Dungeons & Dragons seasons are:

  • Season of Horror - April through June 2026
  • Season of Magic - July through September 2026
  • Season of Champions - October through December 2026

Season of Horror

The Sourcebook that will be serving as an anchor for the Season of Horror is Ravenloft: The Horrors Within. This book will be available to pre-order on April 13th with a release date of June 16th (as has been the trend with recent Dungeons & Dragons digital releases Hero Tier will obtain it a week before, while Master Tier will get it two weeks earlier).

In Ravenloft: The Horrors Within, players will get access to horror-themed subclasses, species, backgrounds, and Dark Gift feats.

For the DM in your life there will be additional content around Darklords and Domains of Dread. Stat blocks and additional information can also be found for important characters to Dungeons & Dragon's horror settings/ Rudolph van Richten is specifically named in the announcement as a potential ally.

On the surface, I'm always a fan of seeing how sub-classes can alter the way I enjoy Dungeons & Dragons, but I also wonder how different the content in Ravenloft: The Horrors Within will be in comparison to Van Richten's Guide to Ravenloft, which was released in 2021.

The other products that will be released in conjunction with the Season of Horror are going to be a 60-card tarokka deck that will be usable with Ravenloft: The Horrors Within, a Horrors Within DM Screen, and a Horrors Within Map Pack. Each will be available for $24.99 and will also be released in June 2026.

Season of Magic

The Season of Magic is going to begin with a Source Book and an Adventure Book.

Arcana Unleashed is a sourcebook meant to be "the ultimate guide for D&D magic enthusiasts." Features of the book include high-magic character creation options and a system for creating magical items that will grow with your character as they level.

The accompanying adventure, Arcana Unleashed: Deadfall, is an adventure that focuses on the Red Wizards as a new Wizard War is brewing on the horizon. The adventure also promises a "deep dive into the pwoerful magical society of Thay.

Both of the above books will release in September 2026.

Also announced for August 2026 are Dungeons & Dragons Reference Card Decks. These decks for spells, magic items, and monsters will give players and DMs a physical card to help organize their gameplay.

This isn't the first time reference cards have been released by Dungeons & Dragons. I was very impressed with their use in the recent Heroes of the Borderlands Starter Set, turning the pen and paper RPG into a bit more of a board game with cards that players can hold to better understand spells and abilities.

Andrew has written Video Game and Entertainment news, reviews, and guides for 10+ years. As Senior Content Manager, he assists in creating and editing… More about Andrew



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/dungeons-dragons-2026-roadmap-promises-seasons-of-horror-and-magic
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
